name: Slavic Magic
description: Slavic Magic encompasses a diverse range of folk beliefs and practices rooted in ancient Slavic paganism and animistic traditions. It emphasizes a deep connection with nature, spirits, and ancestors, seeking to influence the world through ritual, incantation, and divination. The origins of Slavic Magic are intertwined with the agricultural lifestyle and seasonal cycles of the early Slavs, who sought to ensure bountiful harvests, protect their communities, and heal the sick. Practices often involve appeasing or harnessing the power of spirits dwelling in forests, rivers, and homes. Common methods include the use of herbs, amulets, charms, and offerings. Slavic Magic held a significant role in daily life, providing explanations for unexplained phenomena and offering solutions to life's challenges. Over time, elements of Christianity were incorporated, creating a syncretic blend of beliefs and practices that persisted despite religious suppression. Magic was woven into the fabric of society, used for healing, protection, love, and divination, influencing social structures and reinforcing cultural values.
subtitle: Folk magic rooted in Slavic tradition

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: The myth of Svarog striking the primordial stone with his hammer, creating the world and sparking the first fire, highlights the importance of creation and cosmic order. This story emphasizes the blacksmith god's role in shaping reality and giving life to the world.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Perun and Veles
keyMyths.description: The eternal conflict between Perun, the god of thunder and lightning, and Veles, the god of the underworld and cattle, represents the struggle between order and chaos. This myth illustrates the importance of balance and the cyclical nature of life and death.

sources.author: Linda Ivanits
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Russian folk beliefs and practices, including magic and divination.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Russian folk beliefs and practices, including magic and divination.

sources.author: Andreas Johns
sources.work: Baba Yaga: The Ambiguous Mother and Witch of the Russian Folktale
sources.description: Explores the figure of Baba Yaga and her connection to Slavic magic and the spirit world.
sources.text: Explores the figure of Baba Yaga and her connection to Slavic magic and the spirit world.
